mnemonics.title: HEART Framework
mnemonics.content: Remember HEART as 'Happy Elephants Always Run Together' - Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task Success

mnemonics.title: AARRR Pirate Metrics
mnemonics.content: Remember AARRR as 'Acquiring Activists Really Rally Revenue' or 'An Acronym Recalled Repeatedly Remembers Revenue'

mnemonics.title: NPS Calculation
mnemonics.content: Remember NPS as 'Promoters Minus Detractors' - simply subtract percentage of detractors from percentage of promoters

mnemonics.title: SUS Questions
mnemonics.content: SUS has 10 questions - odd questions (1,3,5,7,9) are positive, even questions (2,4,6,8,10) are negative - sum and multiply by 2.5

acronyms.usage: Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend
Measures immediate satisfaction with a product/interaction

acronyms.expansion: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task Success
acronyms.usage: Framework for measuring UX at scale
Pirate Metrics for startup growth measurement
Overall experience of a user with a product/service

visualTips.visualization: Picture a scale from -100 to +100. Promoters (9-10) push positive, Detractors (0-6) push negative. Net = Promoters% - Detractors%
Think of SUS like a thermometer: below 50 is poor, 50-70 is average, above 68 is good, above 80 is excellent
Brain icon for ATTITUDINAL (think), Hand icon for BEHAVIORAL (do) - what users think vs what users do
Picture an inverted pyramid: Visitors → Leads → Customers → Loyal Customers → Promoters - each level narrows

analogies.analogy: NPS is like asking 'Would you invite this friend to your party?' - promoters are guests who bring more guests, detractors are ones who badmouth the party.
CES is like measuring how many doors you must open to reach your destination - fewer doors = easier journey.
Bounce rate is like shop window shoppers who walk in, look around, and leave without buying anything.
HEART is like a doctor's checkup for your product - happiness is mood, engagement is activity level, adoption is new patients, retention is returning patients, task success is recovery rate.
